Since $C^*[0]=0$ and $C^*[-1]=0$, the first term is 0.
$S = \sum_{i=1}^{n-1} \max(0, C^*[i] - C^*[i-1])$.

If we use the definition $C^*[i+1] = \max(0, C^*[i] + N[i] - N[i+1])$ for $i=0, \ldots, n-2$.

$C^*[i+1] - C^*[i] = \max(0, C^*[i] + N[i] - N[i+1]) - C^*[i]$.

If $C^*[i] + N[i] - N[i+1] \ge 0$:

Contribution to $S$: $\max(0, N[i] - N[i+1])$.

If $C^*[i] + N[i] - N[i+1] < 0$:
$C^*[i+1] - C^*[i] = 0 - C^*[i] = -C^*[i]$.
Contribution to $S$: $\max(0, -C^*[i]) = 0$.

$$S = \sum_{i=0}^{n-2} \begin{cases} \max(0, N[i] - N[i+1]) & \text{if } C^*[i] + N[i] - N[i+1] \ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$$
where $C^*$ is calculated iteratively:
$C^*[-1] = 0$
$C^*[i] = \max(0, C^*[i-1] + N[i-1] - N[i])$ for $i=0, \ldots, n-1$.
